title: Audit and correct README claims against reality
purpose: The external review found that README and documentation claims outrun the current implementation — WebSocket streaming documented as shipped (but not routed), 'durable' timers that aren't durable, crash-resume claims that don't work in production. After the IG-001 through IG-005 fixes land, the README needs to accurately reflect what works and what's in progress.
task: Audit the root README.md and crate-level READMEs against the current implementation state. Remove or qualify any claims that aren't yet true. Add honest status indicators for in-progress features.

requirements.title: Root README accuracy
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL audit README.md and ensure every claimed feature is either working in the current build or marked with a clear status indicator (e.g., 'In Progress', 'Planned').
acceptance: No feature is documented as shipped or implied-working that isn't actually functional.
In-progress features are clearly marked with a status indicator.
The feature list matches the current state of the codebase after IG-001 through IG-005.

requirements.title: Getting-started guide validity
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL verify that every command in the getting-started guide works end-to-end and update any that don't.
acceptance: A new user following the guide step-by-step reaches a working workflow.
All commands produce the documented output or the documentation matches actual output.
Prerequisites and environment setup steps are accurate and complete.

requirements.title: API documentation accuracy
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL audit API.md and docs/ for claims about WebSocket streaming, crash recovery, and durable timers, updating them to match post-IG reality.
acceptance: No API endpoint is documented that doesn't exist.
Feature status matches implementation for WebSocket streaming, crash recovery, and durable timers.
Documented endpoint paths, methods, and payloads match the actual server routes.

boundaries: SHALL NOT remove documentation for planned features — only add honest status markers.
SHALL NOT modify code — documentation only.
verification: All commands in getting-started guide execute successfully.
No documentation claims a feature that doesn't work without a status qualifier.
Crate-level READMEs match their crate's current public API.
API.md endpoints match aion-server's actual route table.
